summary: Alphabet is the strongest AI infrastructure play among hyperscalers, with Google Cloud accelerating to 63% growth and a $460B backlog justifying its $180-190B capex commitment; the market has validated this with a 10% surge post-earnings.

Meta is the most vulnerable of the four hyperscalers to continued market skepticism because it lacks a third-party cloud business to validate its massive capex commitment, making its AI spending thesis the weakest among peers.

The oil futures curve is pricing a sharp normalization from current ~$110 Brent to roughly $80 by December 2026 and ~$70 by late 2027, representing either a generational hedging opportunity for energy buyers or a violent correction risk if the backwardation proves wrong.

The S&P 500 is in a constructive trend with the path of least resistance higher into quarter-end, supported by record retail buying flows and favorable July seasonal tailwinds, with 7,610 as the key level to clear.
Front-end Treasury yields face upward pressure with the risk of accelerating higher if the May PCE print surprises to the upside, as the market would move to fully price the 2026 rate hike.
The Fed's updated dot plot signals a hawkish pivot with 9 of 19 participants forecasting a rate hike in 2026, pushing the year-end median Fed Funds rate projection to 3.8% from 3.4%.

The Fed's updated dot plot signals a hawkish shift, with markets increasingly pricing in a rate hike by year-end 2026, driven by upwardly revised inflation forecasts and the Fed's explicit pledge to deliver price stability.

The Fed's hawkish shift under Warsh — with higher dot plot projections and a pledge to deliver price stability — raises the probability of a rate hike by year-end 2026, pressuring rate-sensitive assets and bonds.
Key S&P 500 support sits at the 50-DMA (7,329); a break below this level would signal a meaningful shift in trend, while a break above 7,610 (the record high) would confirm bullish continuation.

A hot May PCE print on June 25 is the primary near-term risk catalyst that could fully price in a 2026 Fed rate hike, driving the dollar and front-end Treasury yields sharply higher while pressuring equities.

Micron (MU) faces asymmetric downside risk into its fiscal Q3 earnings report; a beat may not be sufficient to sustain the stock given elevated expectations and a whisper number above already-high consensus.

timeframe: first week of new quarter (early Q3 2026)

summary: SPCX is viewed bearishly due to accelerating cash burn, a debt issuance decision that signals financial stress rather than strategic discipline, and a $6.4B operating loss in its AI segment that is worsening into 2026.
SPCX (SpaceX) is viewed bearishly near-term following its debt issuance decision; accelerating cash burn and a 5% float create significant downside risks despite IPO enthusiasm.
